things.name: Markt Square
things.description: Bruges' grand central square lined with guild houses, horse-drawn carriages and the iconic Belfry tower

things.name: Belfry of Bruges
things.description: Medieval bell tower soaring 83 metres above the Markt, with 366 steps to panoramic views over the city

things.name: Basilica of the Holy Blood
things.description: Twelfth-century chapel housing a venerated relic believed to contain blood of Christ, a UNESCO masterpiece

things.name: Canal Boat Tours
things.description: Glide through Bruges' UNESCO-listed medieval waterways past weeping willows and chocolate-box merchant houses

things.name: Groeningemuseum
things.description: World-class collection of Flemish Primitive and Belgian art spanning six centuries from Jan van Eyck onwards

things.name: Church of Our Lady
things.description: Gothic brick church with the tallest brick spire in the world, housing Michelangelo's Madonna and Child sculpture

things.name: Begijnhof
things.description: UNESCO-listed tranquil enclave of whitewashed houses founded in 1245 as a religious community for women

things.name: Minnewater
things.description: Romantic 'Lake of Love' at the edge of the Begijnhof, where swans glide beneath willow trees

things.name: Halve Maan Brewery
things.description: Family-owned brewery dating to 1856 producing Brugse Zot beer, with tours through the historic cellars

things.name: Jan van Eyckplein
things.description: Historic trading heart of medieval Bruges where Flemish merchants once gathered, now a picturesque canal-side square
